title: Racosperma subulatum
intro: Racosperma subulatum, also known as the shrub acacia, is a small, thorny shrub native to Australia, characterized by its spiny branches and white or cream-colored flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma subulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tea, tincture, and powder, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.

uses: Racosperma subulatum is a plant species commonly known as the 'Mulga', and it has various medicinal uses in traditional Australian Aboriginal medicine. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore eyes, and skin infections.
